text.tspan: Three views, three questions: what connects, how a turn runs, and what lives inside the app
The five-second view: who uses WonderDrive and which managed systems it depends on
choose a performer, model, question, and next path
resolve identity · enforce ownership and limits
assemble research context · validate · persist · stream
Sites owns the application boundary and server-only secrets.
React / Vinext application · Sign in with ChatGPT seam
The audience-selected model researches and performs the turn.
Built-in web search · structured output · provider-returned sources
No hidden planner, second model, or background agent.
Canonical durable product state: identity, preferences, journeys,
turns, choices, graph edges, research evidence, usage, and snapshots.
Only Sites server routes can access the `DB` binding.
Cloudflare D1 through the Sites-managed DB binding
A sequence view: the same four participants, with time moving downward
action + IDs + idempotency key + expected version
identity · ownership · budget · lease · compact context
selected model + web tools + strict turn schema
safe activity · source allowlist · citations · exactly 2 paths
turn + choices + sources + graph edge + usage + request result
the durable journey version is now authoritative
SSE activity first; visible answer only after commit
A container/component view: which code boundary owns each responsibility
Sites-managed `DB` binding · prepared SQL · D1 batch transactions
optimistic versions · idempotent replay · cost and usage ledgers
IDENTITY identities · preferences · upgrades · starter cache
RESEARCH requests · runs · events · sources · relations
D1 is canonical; the browser has no DB binding
Secrets, prompts, source validation, and D1 access stay server-side.
The infrastructure view: how source becomes a public release and where production configuration lives
Sign in with ChatGPT identity seam
called only from Sites server routes
packaged Drizzle migrations · prepared SQL / batch
CI proves the build; production secrets are injected by the hosting environment and never committed to GitHub.
OPENAI_API_KEY · daily budget limit · Sites-managed DB binding
Architecture intent: one explicit audience action → one bounded foreground turn → one atomic durable result.
